goal: Align model design, training objectives, rollout behavior, pocket-conditioning supervision, chemical-validity constraints, and evaluation gates with the final goal: de novo pocket-conditioned molecular generation that is valid, geometrically plausible, pocket-compatible, diverse, efficient, and claim-safe.
context_summary: The codebase already has separate topology, geometry, and pocket/context encoders; slot decomposition; gated directed cross-modal interaction; flow matching; molecular branch heads; target matching; semantic and leakage probes; staged auxiliary objectives; and raw-vs-processed evaluation instrumentation. The remaining risk is objective alignment: optimizer-visible training still relies heavily on reference-ligand supervised one-step proxy losses, while final generation quality is mostly observed through no-grad rollout diagnostics and downstream repair/evaluation. This plan focuses on closing that gap without collapsing modality boundaries or replacing controlled interaction with naive fusion.
current_state_summary: Q14 hardening completed before this plan. Q15 P0 through P4 are complete: optimizer-visible objective inventory, raw generation quality contracts, bounded rollout training, pocket-ligand interaction supervision, equivariant geometry flow, and chemistry-native generation constraints are implemented/documented. P4 adds generated-logit valence-budget guardrails with over/under logging, bond-length/nonbonded/angle proxies, staged chemistry weights, raw graph repair/conflict metrics, and the contract in docs/q15_chemistry_native_constraints.md. P5 is complete: de novo initialization now supports config-gated bounded multi-sample scaffold/x0 policies with deterministic sample seeds, trainer expansion, evaluation candidate provenance, and rollout reports; explicit pocket-conditioned atom-count and composition prior heads/losses are staged and tested; generation-layer diversity now uses validity-conditioned, permutation-invariant equivalence classes so invalid or atom-order-permuted candidates do not inflate raw uniqueness.
primary_risks: Rollout generation quality is mostly diagnostic and not directly optimizer-facing.
De novo full-flow training still imitates a single reference ligand instead of learning a pocket-conditioned generation distribution.
Pocket-conditioning supervision is too thin if it is limited to per-atom binary contact targets.
The default coordinate flow heads are MLP-based over centered coordinates rather than SE(3)-equivariant by construction.
Chemical validity is partially enforced by graph extraction, repair, or downstream filtering instead of native differentiable objectives.
Auxiliary objectives can dominate or obscure final generation quality without objective-family scale budgets and promotion gates.
Evaluation can overstate readiness if raw-native generation metrics are not separated from repaired or postprocessed metrics.
non_goals: Do not collapse topology, geometry, and pocket/context encoders into one unrestricted fusion encoder.
Do not replace directed gated cross-modal interaction with naive full fusion except as an explicit negative-control ablation.
Do not rewrite core modeling, training, data loading, or evaluation logic into Python.
Do not make unstable mutual-information estimators the central redundancy objective.
Do not hide coordinate repair, valence repair, docking reranking, or backend scoring inside raw model-native metrics.
Do not promote smoke-test execution into benchmark-quality molecular-generation claims.
execution_rules: Complete phases in order unless a later task is required to unblock an earlier one.
Delete a task object from todo.json only after its acceptance_criteria, verification_profile, and completion_evidence are satisfied.
Do not delete a phase until every task in that phase has been completed and removed.
If a task is blocked, keep it in todo.json and add blocker_note with the concrete blocker and proposed next step.
After deleting tasks, update task_count_expected and phase_count so they match remaining tasks and phases.
Keep the crate compiling after each implementation phase.
Prefer Rust implementation and tests for core model, loss, trainer, data, evaluation, and experiment infrastructure.
Preserve separate modality encoders, explicit slot decomposition, directed gated cross-modal interaction, and ablation-friendly config surfaces.
Use targeted tests for touched modules first, then broader verification before removing phase-critical tasks.
Avoid reverting unrelated dirty worktree changes.
When context becomes large, update current_state_summary with completed phase outcomes before continuing.

workflow.execution_order: P0 objective signal audit, P1 optimizer-facing rollout training, P2 pocket-ligand interaction supervision, P3 equivariant geometry flow, P4 chemistry-native constraints, P5 distributional de novo training, P6 objective scale and staging, P7 evaluation ablations and documentation.
workflow.task_retention: Never delete a task until concrete completion evidence exists in the working context.
workflow.task_count_policy: task_count_expected must equal the total number of remaining task objects across all phases.
workflow.phase_count_policy: phase_count must equal the number of phases that still contain task objects.
workflow.implementation_policy: Prefer small vertical slices that add config, implementation, metrics, and tests together. Keep every slice runnable and ablation-friendly.

task_metadata_schema.id: Stable task id. Remove the entire task object only after completion.
task_metadata_schema.title: Short task name.
task_metadata_schema.task_type: audit | contract | design | implementation | validation | documentation | configuration
task_metadata_schema.depends_on: List of task ids that should be completed first.
task_metadata_schema.problem: Concrete issue the task protects against.
task_metadata_schema.action: Implementation, audit, validation, or documentation work to perform.
task_metadata_schema.acceptance_criteria: Checks required before deleting the task.
task_metadata_schema.completion_evidence: Concrete evidence required before removing the task from todo.json.
